children.label: A
children.text: a decision by an accrediting entity with respect to the accreditation of an agency or approval of a person under subchapter II;

children.label: B
children.text: the relinquishment of parental rights or the giving of parental consent relating to the adoption of a child in a case subject to the Convention; or

children.label: C
children.text: a decision or action of any entity performing a central authority function; or
engages another person as an agent, whether in the United States or in a foreign country, who in the course of that agency takes any of the actions described in paragraph (1) or (2),

children.heading: Authority of Attorney General
children.label: 1 Authority of Attorney General
children.text: The Attorney General may bring a civil action to enforce subsection (a) against any person in any United States district court.

children.heading: Factors to be considered in imposing penalties
children.label: 2 Factors to be considered in imposing penalties
children.text: In imposing penalties the court shall consider the gravity of the violation, the degree of culpability of the defendant, and any history of prior violations by the defendant.

children.heading: Criminal penalties
children.label: c Criminal penalties
children.text: Whoever knowingly and willfully violates paragraph (1) or (2) of subsection (a) shall be subject to a fine of not more than $250,000, imprisonment for not more than 5 years, or both.
